commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer label and broad transcript persistence risk call for human maintainer review; I did not find a narrow automated repair to queue.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain regression was found; the patch does not change dependencies, workflows, secrets, or permissions, and it preserves hook redaction/blocking paths in tests. commands.review_summary:
The branch centralizes user-turn transcript construction and persistence across Gateway chat.send, auto-reply dispatch, CLI/Pi runtimes, Codex app-server mirroring, and transcript idempotency tests.

PR surface: Source +804, Tests +1839. Total +2643 across 28 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was established in this read-only review. The source paths and added tests make the media/transcript persistence failure plausible, but the Control UI/TUI behavior was not rerun here.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer label and broad transcript persistence risk call for human maintainer review; I did not find a narrow automated repair to queue.

target.title: refactor: centralize user turn transcript persistence
target.body:
## Summary

Centralizes user-turn transcript shaping and persistence so text/media turns from Gateway, channel reply dispatch, CLI runtimes, Pi runtimes, and Codex app-server mirroring use one shared recorder path instead of scattered transcript mutation/rewrite logic.

Key changes:
- Adds a shared user-turn transcript module for:
  - building persisted user messages
  - preserving media display metadata
  - applying `before_message_write`
  - append/update emission
  - retry/replay dedupe
  - runtime/fallback persistence coordination
- Removes the old WebChat media transcript rewrite path and prepares media metadata before persistence instead.
- Removes duplicate raw `userTurnInput` / CLI `userTurnTranscript` handoff surfaces in favor of the shared recorder.
- Keeps model input construction on the existing runtime path; this only changes transcript persistence/projection.
- Handles runtime-owned persistence, Gateway fallback persistence, before-agent-run blocked prompts, CLI hook timing, and Codex app-server mirrored prompts through the same user-turn persistence contract.

## Transcript Path Gains

- User-turn media is now persisted before/with the user transcript message instead of being patched in later by scanning and rewriting transcript rows.
- Gateway no longer has to find "the matching recent user message" by text to attach media metadata, which removes a fragile and potentially expensive post-run rewrite path.
- Runtime-owned user persistence and Gateway fallback persistence now coordinate through one recorder, so retries/fallbacks can tell whether the user turn was already persisted before writing again.
- Text, media, idempotency key, and blocked-run state now travel together as one prepared user-turn record instead of being reconstructed separately by Gateway, CLI, and runtime callers.
- Duplicate user-turn appends are avoided with stable idempotency keys for replay/retry paths.
- `before_agent_run` blocked prompts are explicitly marked on the recorder, so fallback persistence does not leak blocked prompt text.
- Model input is unchanged: the runtime still receives the existing prompt/images/context path; this refactor only changes transcript persistence and UI reload projection.
- The old WebChat media path required delayed transcript mutation after the agent run. The new path makes the persisted transcript row correct at append time, so Control UI reloads can render the same media metadata without waiting for a later repair.

## Performance Note

The normal runtime-owned media user-turn path is faster because media metadata is now prepared before persistence instead of patched into the transcript later by searching/indexing and rewriting a prior user row.

Generated large-session benchmark, 7 measured iterations with 2 warmups:

- `1k` entries / `218 KB`: `3.35 ms` old rewrite -> `1.88 ms` prepared append (`1.8x`)
- `5k` entries / `1.1 MB`: `11.03 ms` old rewrite -> `6.25 ms` prepared append (`1.8x`)
- `10k` entries / `2.2 MB`: `23.42 ms` old rewrite -> `12.35 ms` prepared append (`1.9x`)
- `25k` entries / `5.6 MB`: `60.57 ms` old rewrite -> `28.38 ms` prepared append (`2.1x`)
- `50k` entries / `11.3 MB`: `124.12 ms` old rewrite -> `58.14 ms` prepared append (`2.1x`)

This benchmark covers the normal runtime-owned media user-turn persistence path. Gateway fallback persistence still keeps an idempotency scan to avoid duplicate user turns on replay, so it is comparable to the old scan/rewrite path rather than the optimized path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: User messages with media could lose their display media after the run finished or after transcript reload because persistence and live UI projection were handled by different paths.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw runtime branch with Control UI and TUI live testing during development.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts \
  src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts \
  src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/sessions/user-turn-transcript.test.ts

node --import tsx .artifacts/user-turn-persistence-benchmark.mjs

git diff --check
```

Evidence after fix:
- `7 passed`, `507 tests passed` for the focused runtime/Gateway/CLI/media suite.
- `1 passed`, `24 tests passed` for the shared user-turn transcript module.
- Generated benchmark shows the normal runtime-owned media user-turn path at `1.8x` to `2.1x` faster than the old post-run rewrite path across `1k` to `50k` transcript entries.
- `git diff --check` passed.

Observed result after fix:
- User-turn transcript media metadata is prepared before persistence and passed through the shared recorder.
- Runtime-owned persistence remains the normal path.
- Gateway fallback only persists when runtime persistence did not happen or cannot happen.
- Duplicate user-turn appends are deduped by idempotency key.
- Blocked `before_agent_run` messages do not leak raw prompt content into transcript persistence.
